title: FigureArtifactV1
description:
What Cortexel EMITS. Every field here is library-generated; none is caller-settable.

An artifact records what was validated, derived, decided, disclosed, and rendered. In the 0.9 development writer it binds the normative SVG payload; exact table rows are returned separately in memory and are not yet content-addressed by the artifact. A detached artifact reader/verifier and canonical table sidecar remain unimplemented, so output-integrity verification is a reserved assurance level rather than a claim made by this writer.

Parsed, structurally valid, and semantically valid are distinguished and never called `scientifically true`, `reproduced`, `paper verified`, or `calibrated`. A structurally and semantically valid figure can still be built on incorrect, incomplete, biased, or mislabelled source data.

buildIdentity.description: Every identity axis, named. `specVersion`-style bare tuples are gone: a number is meaningless without the axis it belongs to.

sourceRevision.description: The current writer has no release-stamping producer. A real release revision is added only together with that executable build path and its evidence.

release.description: The current writer emits development artifacts only. The schema rejects a release claim no implemented producer can make.

canonicalRequest.description: The structurally valid per-skill request after conservative canonicalization. Its skill.revision is always the resolved installed revision; this is the artifact's sole skill-revision stamp. The independent renderer identity remains under render.rendererId/render.rendererRevision. This schema re-checks the closed request shape as an emission postcondition. It does not recompute requestDigest and is not a detached integrity verifier.

inputAssurance.description: What the input boundary could actually certify — which is not the same for raw JSON text and for an already-materialized JavaScript value.

duplicateKeys.description: Duplicate object members can only be detected while scanning raw text. Once JSON.parse has run, one value has already silently won. An API that accepts a JavaScript value therefore reports the LOWER assurance rather than implying a check it cannot perform.

validation.description: What the current successful writer actually checked. Structural, semantic, and resource validation passed; no independent reference oracle is executed in Artifact 1.0.

checkedRuleIds.description: Ordered unique registry rule identifiers exercised by semantic validation. This is a rule-kind summary, not a per-parameter invocation log; a skill may apply one rule identifier at multiple catalog-declared pointers.

derivation.description: Every transformation Cortexel performed, in order, with its inputs and outputs bound by digest. A caller cannot append an operation to a certified artifact.

receipt.description: Exactly what the operation did: grouping, boundaries, denominators, and how many observations were excluded and why. An excluded event is always counted; it is never silently dropped.

inputDigest.description: RFC 8785 SHA-256 over the global-context digest and ordered series-input digest carriers in sourceIndex order, under the operation-input suffix of the declared digestDomain.

outputDigest.description: RFC 8785 SHA-256 over the complete closed receipt, including global context, per-series input digests, closed prepared-view receipts, transforms, witness records, materialization subdigest carriers, and state flags, under the operation-output suffix of the declared digestDomain. Artifact 1.0 omits the intermediate prepared-view, ordinal, witness-observation, and materialization preimages, so the post-schema gate recomputes this enclosing wrapper but not those inner subdigests.

inputDigest.description: RFC 8785 SHA-256 over the global-context digest and ordered series-input digest carriers in sourceIndex order, under the operation-input suffix of the declared digestDomain.

outputDigest.description: RFC 8785 SHA-256 over the complete closed receipt, including global context, per-series input digests, closed prepared-view receipts, transforms, witness records, materialization subdigest carriers, and state flags, under the operation-output suffix of the declared digestDomain. Artifact 1.0 omits the intermediate prepared-view, ordinal, witness-observation, and materialization preimages, so the post-schema gate recomputes this enclosing wrapper but not those inner subdigests.

budgetDecision.description: The current successful writer only emits a full accepted figure. Rejections are FigureFailure values, not artifacts; compaction becomes a new artifact-contract revision only when its producer, bound complete-table bytes, and verifier exist.

provenance.description: A caller's source declaration and Cortexel's local assurances remain strictly separate. Artifact 1.0 has no signature verifier and carries an explicitly empty attestations slot, so absence can never be mistaken for source authentication.

requestDigest.description: SHA-256 over the RFC 8785 canonical request. This is the seed domain used by deterministic SVG ids; integrity verification must recompute it from canonicalRequest.

assurances.description: The exact ordered assurance records emitted by Artifact 1.0. They certify local checks only; none authenticates the producer or scientific source.

attestations.description: Artifact 1.0 has no signature/attestation verifier and therefore emits exactly no attestation records.

accessibility.description: EVIDENCE, not a boolean. There is deliberately no `accessible: true`: a conformance claim about a page Cortexel does not own would be a claim it cannot support.

summary.description: Deterministic non-interpretive description. It contains every mandatory disclosure verbatim so the SVG description and visible footer carry the same honesty statements.

tablePolicy.description: Every table row is returned in memory alongside this artifact. Rows are not embedded in the artifact JSON and are never excerpted, sampled, or summarized; an over-limit request is refused.

tableBinding.description: The artifact binds ordered column keys and row count, not cell values or row bytes. Table-content integrity is not verifiable from FigureArtifactV1.

tableColumns.description: The exact ordered column keys of the returned in-memory table. These keys are bound into the logical artifact and permit schema/order reconciliation, but the artifact does not bind the table row bytes in this version.

disclosures.description: Derived ONLY from machine-checkable artifact facts through the closed disclosure-rule registry. A caller cannot add, remove, reorder, or reword one. The exact same text appears in the artifact, in the visible SVG footer, in the SVG accessible description, and in the table metadata — a test asserts all four agree.

outputs.description: The one normative payload whose exact bytes are bound by this artifact. The artifact JSON itself is covered by artifactDigest and cannot list its own bytes without a self-hash cycle. Sidecars, PNG, and detached provenance outputs are absent from Artifact 1.0 and require a later contract revision with real producers and verification.

artifactDigest.description: SHA-256 over the RFC 8785 canonical bytes of this artifact with `artifactDigest` itself excluded. No timestamp or other volatile value enters this domain.

tracePreparationWindow.description: The exact window applied by one preparation view after unit conversion. The boolean preserves the executable final-edge rule rather than translating it back into request prose.

traceConversionReceipt.description: The existing Cortexel ConversionReceipt shape. A rounded binary64 factor is accompanied by the exact rational scale that reproduces it; an identity conversion is represented by null in the enclosing transforms object, not by a from==to receipt.

weightMemberPreparationBatchTransforms.description: The complete fixed transform inventory for one raw weight member. Every applicable unit change uses an exact ConversionReceipt; a null says that field was absent, not rendered, or already in the target unit. Weight preparation performs no normalization.

uncertainty.description: Revision 2 raw edge series semantically require uncertainty.kind=none, so no uncertainty conversion can occur.

declaredWeightAggregatePreparationBatchT.description: The complete fixed transform inventory for a caller-declared aggregate. Only its supplied time and uncertainty channels can be converted; the aggregate is prepared in its own value unit, while member interval, initial-state, normalization, and bound transforms are impossible. Those fields are explicitly null.

value.description: A caller-declared aggregate is prepared in its own declared value unit in revision 1, so no value conversion can occur.

compartmentPreparationBatchTransforms.description: The complete fixed transform inventory for a compartment series. Only its time, value, and common declared uncertainty channels can be converted; weight-only and normalization transforms are impossible and therefore explicitly null.

tracePreparationEventMaterialization.description: A complete binding of the event-updated run materialization over the effective intersection of the declared analysis window and recorded interval. The full digest binds the source series before any derived-membership or display-selection filter; the displayed digest independently binds the presentation-eligible materialization, or the explicit null used when that source series is not painted. Off-display observations that affect state are bound separately by the state view and context-witness receipts.

tracePreparationBatchView.description: One closed prepared-view receipt. The producer computes its subdigests, while the post-schema gate checks receipt-internal count conservation and the enclosing batch wrapper. Artifact 1.0 omits the prepared-observation and retained-ordinal preimages, so that gate does not recompute the view subdigests; this schema closes their carriers and numeric domains.

inputSourceCount.description: Number of source observations before duplicate handling and window filtering.

retainedSourceCount.description: Number of retained source observations, preserving multiplicity even when a duplicate-time group becomes one prepared output row.

outputRowCount.description: Number of prepared output observations after duplicate handling and window filtering.

missingRetainedSourceCount.description: Number of retained source values that are null, preserving multiplicity inside duplicate-time aggregates.

duplicateGroupCount.description: Duplicate-time groups across the complete input before window filtering. A group wholly outside the view window still contributes.

reordered.description: Whether stable time ordering changed the complete input order before window filtering, including observations later excluded from this view.

retainedSourceOrdinalDigest.description: Digest of the ordered source-ordinal groups in exact prepared-output order. Group boundaries are preserved, so a duplicate-time aggregate cannot collide with the same ordinals split across separate output rows.

outputDigest.description: Digest under the view-output suffix of the operation digestDomain. It binds the exact prepared observations and any aligned uncertainty arrays, not only counts.

tracePreparationContextWitness.description: A producer-side commitment to an out-of-display observation that influences event-held state. The index identifies the observation in the producer's state view, but Artifact 1.0 carries only that index and an observation digest. The post-schema gate checks the index against the declared state-view row count; it does not re-execute state preparation or recompute the omitted observation preimage.

consultedByDerivedAggregate.description: True exactly when this off-display state observation was consumed by the derived weight aggregate; false identifies a presentation-only event-held-state witness.

tracePreparationContextWitnesses.description: At most one carry-in and one look-ahead observation can affect a clipped piecewise-constant display view.

compartmentSeriesIdentity.description: A structured tuple avoids the collisions produced by joining caller identifiers with a delimiter that the identifier grammar itself permits.

inputDigest.description: Digest under the series-input suffix of the operation digestDomain. It binds the raw series and every global request carrier that controls its display/state preparation.

views.description: A raw weight member always records the clipped display preparation followed by the complete recorded-interval state preparation.

inputDigest.description: Digest under the series-input suffix of the operation digestDomain, binding the caller-declared aggregate and its preparation controls.

views.description: A declared aggregate has no separately recorded member-state interval; its single exact display preparation is the auditable caller carrier.

inputDigest.description: Digest under the series-input suffix of the operation digestDomain, binding the raw compartment series and every global request carrier that controls its preparation.

weightMemberTracePreparationBatchReceipt.description: The homogeneous batch receipt for canonical raw edge-series request order. Runtime postconditions require seriesCount to equal seriesReceipts.length, sourceIndex to be contiguous, and structured identities to be unique.

weightMemberIndividualPreparationBatchEn.description: An individual-only artifact may paint an initial state, but no aggregate exists that could consume an initial state or consult an off-display context witness.

declaredWeightAggregateTracePreparationB.description: The single-entry batch receipt for a caller-declared preaggregate.

weightTracePreparationBatchReceipt.description: Exactly one homogeneous canonical weight request carrier family.

weightAggregateObservation.description: Only source observations that the aggregate implementation can evaluate without interpolation or a false hold assumption.

weightAggregateEvaluation.description: The three non-interpolating evaluation carriers implemented by the revision-4 aggregate.

weightAggregateParameters.description: The closed revision-4 scientific parameters plus the exact preparation-batch chain identity.

weightAggregateUncertaintyOutput.description: The exact bounded descriptive-spread carrier included in the revision-4 scientific output digest.

weightAggregateOutput.description: The exact bounded scientific output whose RFC 8785 digest is scientificOutputDigest. This carrier permits local integrity recomputation without claiming that the source data or scientific question is correct.

weightAggregateReceipt.description: Closed revision-4 aggregate receipt. Equality of the repeated chain digest, selected-member/count equations, conversion correctness, and digest recomputation are executable emission postconditions beyond JSON Schema.

evaluationCount.description: A successful artifact cannot exceed the skill's immutable 500-row complete-table limit; aggregate rows are part of that same table.

stateWitnessDigest.description: Revision-4 domain-separated digest of the preceding batch's canonical-order context-witness projection filtered to consultedByDerivedAggregate=true.

compartmentAggregateParameters.description: The closed revision-4 explicit-selection aggregate parameters plus the exact preparation-batch chain identity.

traceAggregateOutputUnits.description: The time and scalar-value units omitted by the legacy inner scientific-output digest and bound by the revision-4 domain-separated operation output.

compartmentAggregateReceipt.description: Closed revision-4 aggregate receipt. Count/array equality, repeated chain-digest equality, and digest recomputation remain executable emission postconditions.

compartmentTracePreparationBatchReceipt.description: Runtime postconditions require seriesCount to equal seriesReceipts.length and sourceIndex to be the zero-based contiguous canonical request order.
